frameworks.name: policy compression
frameworks.one_line: Context-dependent policies stored as bounded-codelength internal representations

frameworks.name: information-theoretic cognitive control
frameworks.one_line: Cognitive costs framed as information-theoretic codelength of policies

frameworks.name: rate-distortion
frameworks.one_line: Bounded capacity for storing context-action mappings

frameworks.name: neural dimensionality
frameworks.one_line: Code space for complex policies constrained by neural state-space dimensionality

core_question: How are the costs of context-dependent decisions explained by information-theoretic compression of policies?
key_assumptions: Brain has limited capacity to store context-dependent policies
Policies are compressed to representations with bounded codelength
Actions are decoded by sequentially inspecting code bits
Longer codes take more time to decode
manipulations: Response deadline imposed on rule-based action selection
Rule-based action selection task analyzed with EEG
Policy complexity measured as information-theoretic quantity
key_findings: Policy complexity increased with longer response deadlines
Higher policy complexity associated with higher accuracy
Higher policy complexity associated with lower behavioral variability
Policy complexity correlated with neural dimensionality measure
interpretations: Cognitive control costs reflect information-theoretic compression bounds on policies
Complex policies require higher neural dimensionality to encode
